_comment: Updated annotation — JBI Case Series checklist for Garcia et al. 2026 case series

article.title: Synergistic use of robotic navigational platform and ultrathin bronchoscopy in diagnostic yield optimization for cavitary lung lesions: a case series

study_type.notes: Retrospective case series of 6 consecutive patients with cavitary lung lesions evaluated using combined robotic and ultrathin bronchoscopy.

1.notes: Clear inclusion criteria: patients aged ≥18 with cavitary lung lesions requiring tissue/microbiological diagnosis; pregnant patients excluded; consecutive enrollment of qualifying patients

2.notes: Standardized measurement: cavitary lesion diagnosis confirmed radiologically; ultrathin bronchoscopy performed using standardized Vathin 3.2-mm OD bronchoscope; tissue diagnosis via biopsy following standard protocol

3.notes: Valid diagnostic methods: lesion identification via CT imaging; ultrathin flexible bronchoscopy validated technique; robotic platform (Ion™) navigation confirmed via fluoroscopy; tissue samples for histology/microbiology

4.notes: Consecutive inclusion: 'six consecutive patients with cavitary lung lesions' explicitly stated; prospective enrollment period (1-month); no selection bias from non-consecutive sampling

5.notes: Complete inclusion: all eligible consecutive patients meeting criteria included in analysis; no post-hoc exclusions; n=6 total enrolled and analyzed

6.notes: Limited demographic reporting: case-by-case presentation provided but no formal demographics table; age, sex inferred from case descriptions but not explicitly summarized

7.notes: Clinical information: presenting complaint (cavitary lesions), prior imaging, suspected diagnoses documented; clinical context for each patient provided in case descriptions

8.notes: Outcomes clearly reported: successful navigation in 6/6 (100%); enhanced diagnosis with ultrathin bronchoscopy in 2/4 robotic cases; 1 transient fever (resolved with antibiotics); 0 major complications; 3-month clinical improvement in all 6

9.notes: Site demographics: Los Angeles General Medical Center; tertiary academic medical center; endoscopy suite setting specified; single institution, single operator

10.notes: Statistical analysis: descriptive analysis appropriate for case series; proportions reported (6/6 successful, 2/6 enhanced diagnosis); frequency counts and percentages used
